posts.author: kinga (Kinga Kubiak)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I would like to suggest an option to customize the “Thank You” end page based on the user’s answer. For instance, if the question requires a “yes” or “no” answer, and a “no” answer means the user cannot proceed with the form, then the “thank you” end page after a “no” answer should express regret and state that further support is not possible.

If they answer “yes”, and move on to the second questions, and in that second question the user answers “no” again to check if they are from UK, then the form should end and the page should mention that support is only available for people from the UK.

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, not sure if this fully solves what your asking but FormFlow has customisable thank you messages and redirect URLs built into the submission settings. You can set different messages for different scenarios.

It also has something called Forms Routing where you can show completely different forms from a single link based on conditions from your item fields. So depending on what stage someone is at they could get a different form with a different end message. Might work for your use case.
